message:
feat(rchd): wire cancellation metadata into history and orchestrator cleanup (bd-1yt6)

This commit connects the new BuildCancellationMetadata types to the
daemon's cancellation orchestrator and build history subsystems,
enabling deterministic structured records for every cancelled build.

history.rs changes:
- Extract take_active_build() as a public method that atomically
  claims an active build from the active map. This becomes the
  idempotency/ownership gate: whichever codepath (normal completion
  vs cancellation) claims the active build first is responsible for
  deterministic cleanup and final record write.
- Add record_cancelled_build() that accepts a claimed ActiveBuildState
  and optional BuildCancellationMetadata, writes a cancelled record
  (exit_code 130) with the metadata attached, and pushes it into
  completed history.
- Refactor cancel_active_build() to delegate to take_active_build()
  + record_cancelled_build(), now accepting an optional cancellation
  metadata parameter.
- finish_active_build() also refactored to use take_active_build()
  internally, eliminating duplicated lock logic.
- All test fixture BuildRecord constructions gain `cancellation: None`.
- New test: test_cancel_active_build_records_cancellation_metadata
  verifies the full round-trip of cancellation metadata through
  history (including operation_id, escalation_stage, worker_health).

cancellation.rs changes:
- Add CancellationWorkerHealthSnapshot internal struct for capturing
  worker state at cancellation completion time.
- Add helper functions:
  - cancellation_operation_id(): Generates stable "cancel-{id}" strings
  - worker_status_label(): Maps WorkerStatus enum to stable string labels
  - worker_health_for_history(): Converts internal snapshot to the
    shared BuildCancellationWorkerHealth type
  - push_decision_stage(): Deduplicating append for decision path
  - cancellation_decision_path(): Reconstructs the ordered state
    transitions from a CancellationRecord (handles normal, force,
    and escalation paths)
  - cancellation_escalation_stage(): Determines the highest escalation
    label (term/remote_kill/sigkill) from record state
- capture_worker_health_snapshot(): Async method that reads live worker
  status, speed score, slot usage, and pressure assessment from the
  worker pool.
- run_cleanup() overhauled:
  - Uses take_active_build() as the ownership gate instead of the
    old cancel_active_build() which combined claim+record.
  - Builds full BuildCancellationMetadata from the record state.
  - Calls record_cancelled_build() with the metadata so the completed
    history record carries all cancellation details.
  - Metrics (dec_active_builds, inc_build_total) now fire only after
    successful record write, not before.
  - Event emission enriched with operation_id, cancel_origin,
    decision_path, escalation_stage, history_cancelled, and
    worker_health fields.
- Rustfmt cleanup: several multi-line argument lists and match arms
  reformatted for consistency.
- New tests:
  - test_cancel_inflight_build_records_metadata: Verifies cancellation
    of an in-flight build produces a history record with correct
    metadata (origin, reason_code, operation_id, final_state,
    history_cancelled).
  - test_cancel_after_completion_returns_error_post_completion_race:
    Verifies that cancelling an already-completed build returns error
    (simulates the completion/cancel race condition).
  - test_repeated_cancel_after_completion_is_deterministic: First
    cancel succeeds, second returns error (idempotency).
  - test_cancellation_decision_path_term_only: Term-only path.
  - test_cancellation_decision_path_remote_kill: Term + remote kill.
  - test_cancellation_decision_path_sigkill_escalation: Full escalation.
  - test_cancellation_decision_path_failed_before_remote_kill: Failure
    before remote kill stage.
  - test_cancellation_decision_path_force_cancel: Force cancel skips
    term, goes straight to escalated + remote kill.
  - test_cancellation_operation_id_format: Format contract.
  - test_record helper function added for concise test setup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
